Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

TCL 06, 42 [Ištar rituals][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
oo NaN  (start of obverse missing)
o 1'o 1'

[x] x ina KISAL [...]

(o 1') [...] ... in the forecourt [...]

o 2'2'

[a]-⸢na⸣ KISAL.MAḪ ⸢E₁₁⸣ [...]

(o 2') goes down to the main court. [...]

o 3'3'

[x x] ⸢d⸣A.DA.PA₃ [...]

(o 3') [...] Adapa [...]

o 4'4'

[x x] x E₂.UL.MAŠ [...]

(o 4') [...] ... the Eulmaš [...]

o 5'5'

[x lu₂KUR].GAR.RA TA e₂SAG [...]

(o 5') [...] the kurgarrû cultic performer [...] from the Reš temple.

o 6'6'

dŠA₃.GE.PA₃.DA daš₂-ka-⸢aʾ?⸣-[i?-tu? ...]

(o 6') Šagepada, Aškaʾitu, [...]

o 7'7'

x ⸢d⸣GALGA.MU.NA.SUM.MU dIGI.DU dx [...]

(o 7') ..., Galgamunasumu, Palil, ... [...]

o 8'8'

[dURU₃]—INIM-su 2 dURI₃.GAL u₃ 3 daḫ-[la-mat ...]

(o 8') Uṣur-amassu, 2 divine standards and 3 divine Aramaeans [...]

o 9'9'

[dki?]-⸢li⸣-li u₃ dba-ri-ri-i-tu₄ TA E₂.LIL₂.⸢LA₂?⸣ [...]

(o 9') the Kilili and Bariritu demons [...] from the Elila.

o 10'10'

[x x] 4 UR.MAḪ.LU₂.U₁₈.LU TA E₂ dLAMMA.EDIN dDUMU.MUNUS-MEŠ—E₂.AN.NA ⸢u₃⸣ [...]

(o 10') [...], 4 lion-men from the temple of Lammaedin, the Marat-Eana and [...] and Lammasiga arise and go down to the Kisalšaba court and [...] in [...].

o 11'11'

⸢u₃⸣ dLAMMA.SIG₅.GA ZI-MEŠ-nim-ma a-na KISAL.ŠA₃.BA E₁₁-MEŠ-ma ⸢ina?⸣ [...]

o 12'12'

⸢d⸣ši—la-bat₃ dIGI.BAR.LU₂.TI dKA.BI.LU₂.SIG₅ dA.DA d⸢E₂⸣.[SA.PAR₃]

(o 12') Ši-labat, Igibarluti, Kabilusig, Ada, Esapar, Ninhenuna, Enmeurana and Šešantur, Belet-ayyaki of Udannu, Šarrat-šame and Sadarnuna, Ninimma, Šarrat-parakki, Mišaru and Išartu arise from their seats and, as the Sun appears, all the gods take up position in front of Ištar. The king performs the installation of the enu-priest. He puts on his pure garments.

o 13'13'

⸢d⸣NIN.ḪE₂.NUN.NA dEN.UR.AN.NA u₃ dŠEŠ.AN.⸢TUR⸣ dNIN—E₂.AN.NA ša₂ u₄-⸢dan⸣-[nu]

o 14'14'

⸢d⸣šar-rat—ANe u₃ dSA.DAR₃.NUN.NA dNIN.IMMA₃ dšar-rat—BARA₂ dmi-[šar?-u?]

o 15'15'

u₃ di-šar-tu₄ TA šub-ti-šu₂-nu ZI-MEŠ-nim-⸢ma⸣ ki-ma dUTU KURḫa DINGIR-MEŠ nap-ḫar-šu₂-[nu]

o 16'16'

a-na pa-ni dINANA LA₂aṣ LUGAL IL₂ut EN.NA DU₃uš til-le-e-šu₂ eb-bu-tu il-lab-⸢biš⸣

o 17'17'

x [...] ⸢dša₂-ra-ḫi-i-tu₄⸣ DUak-ma uš₄-ken dša₂-ra-ḫi-i-tu₄ ZI-am it-⸢ti⸣ [x]

(o 17') Šarrahitu goes ... [...] and prostrates herself. Šarrahitu arises. [...] with [...]. The gold carrying pole for the interior is given to Ištar. [...]

o 18'18'

[...] ⸢giš⸣tal-lu KU₃.SI₂₂ E₂-a-ni a-na ⸢dINANA SUMin⸣ [...]

o 19'19'

[... KU₄?-MEŠ?]-⸢ma⸣ GUBzi-zu ⸢nap-tan u₃ uzušu-me-e ḫa⸣-[an-ṭu-tu]

(o 19') [...] enter and stand. He presents the meal and the quickly-cooked roast meat [...] to Ištar. He presents the meal and the quickly-cooked roast meat [...] to Nanaya and Beltu-ša-Reš.

o 20'20'

[... a]-⸢na⸣ dINANA u₂-qar-rab nap-tan u₃ uzušu-me-e ḫa-an-ṭu-[tu]

o 21'21'

[... a]-na dna-na-a u₃ dGAŠAN—ša₂—SAG u₂-qar-⸢rab⸣

o 22'22'

[... a]-na IGI DINGIR-MEŠ ma-la ina KISAL u₂-še-te₉-⸢eq⸣

(o 22') He passes [...] on to as many gods as (are) in the forecourt.

o 23'23'

[... a]-⸢na⸣ IGI dINANA-MEŠ ša₂ ina KISAL u₂-še-te₉-⸢eq⸣

(o 23') He passes [...] on to the goddesses who (are) in the forecourt.

o 24'24'

[... d]⸢na⸣-na-a u₃ dGAŠAN—ša₂—SAG i-red-de-e

(o 24') He pours [...] Nanaya and Beltu-ša-Reš.

o 25'25'

⸢dNIN.IGI-MIN.ZI.BAR⸣.[RA ...] ⸢ŠU⸣-MIN dINANA DABbat-u₃-ma lu₂MAŠ.MAŠ-MEŠ lu₂GALA-MEŠ u lu₂NAR-MEŠ

(o 25') Ninigizibara [...] take the hands of Ištar, and the mašmaššu-priests, the kalû-priests and the musicians bow down. Ištar sits on the seat in the area between the curtains which (is) at the gate of the cella.

o 26'26'

u₂-šar-ru-u₂ dINANA ina muḫ-⸢ḫi šub⸣-ti ša₂ bi-rit šid-di ša₂ ina KA₂ pa-pa-ḫa TUŠab

o 27'27'

DINGIR-MEŠ nap-ḫar-šu₂-nu ša₂ KISAL a-na pa-ni-šu₂ LA₂ṣa ma-aq-qu-u₂ KU₃.SI₂₂ i-red-de-e-ma

(o 27') All the gods in the forecourt take up position in front of her. He pours (from) a gold libation vessel and raises the water for the hands. He strews on top of the place for presenting offerings and sets out a merdītu-offering, an ox and a ram, in front of her. He places the heart of the ox and the head of the ram on top of the place for presenting offerings, and he turns upside down over the heart a gold malītu-vessel (full) of maṣhatu-flour. He pours the libation vessel (full) of wine over the [...] the ram. He raises the water for the hands towards Ištar and sprinkles the king (and) the people. The sceptre [...] ... stands beside the Dais of Destinies.

o 28'28'

A-MEŠ ŠU-MIN IL₂ši ina muḫ-ḫi KI SISKUR₂ GABA.RI i-sar-raq-ma

o 29'29'

SISKUR₂ mer-de-e-ti GU₄ u₃ UDU.NITA₂ ina pa-ni-šu₂ i-red-de uzulib₃-bi ša₂ GU₄ u₃ SAG.DU ša₂ UDU.NITA₂

o 30'30'

ina muḫ-ḫi KI SISKUR₂ GABA.RI i-šak-kan-[ma] ma-lit KU₃.SI₂₂ ša₂ ZI₃.MAD.GA₂ a-na muḫ-ḫi lib₃-bi

o 31'31'

i-saḫ-ḫap ma-aq-⸢qu-u₂ GEŠTIN⸣ a-na ⸢muḫ⸣-[ḫi x x] ⸢UDU.NITA₂⸣ i-red-de A-MEŠ ŠU-MIN a-na dINANA

o 32'32'

IL₂-ma LUGAL UN-MEŠ u₂-lap-pat ⸢giš⸣U₃.[LUḪ ...] x ina DA BARA₂—NAM-MEŠ GUB[za]

o 33'33'

LUGAL ma-aq-qu-u₂ KU₃.SI₂₂ a-na pa-ni d⸢INANA⸣ [...] ⸢ŠU⸣-MIN dINANA

(o 33') The king [...] the gold libation vessel before Ištar. [...] take the hands of Ištar, and Nanaya and Beltu-ša-Reš [...] ....

o 34'34'

DABbat-MEŠ-ma dna-na-a u₃ d⸢GAŠAN⸣—[ša₂—SAG ...] x

o 35'35'

dINANA DUak-ma ina muḫ-ḫi BARA₂—NAM-MEŠ ⸢ša₂?⸣ [...]

(o 35') Ištar goes and [...] on the Dais of Destinies of [...]

o 36'36'

[...] ⸢MA?⸣ NIG₂ ⸢E₃? a⸣-na ⸢lib₃⸣-bi AB [...]

(o 36') [...] ... [...]

Reverse
rr NaN  (start of reverse missing)
r 1'r 1'

dx [...]

(r 1') ... [...].

r 2'2'

EGIR-šu₂ 2 dURI₃.GAL 3 daḫ-la-mat dx [...]

(r 2') Next the 2 divine standards, the 3 divine Aramaeans, ... [...], Kabilusig, Ada and Esapar ... [...].

r 3'3'

dKA.BI.LU₂.SIG₅ dA.DA u dE₂.SA.PAR₃ x [...]

r 4'4'

EGIR-šu₂ MUNUS ša₂ SAG KALAtu₂ dURU₃—INIM-su ⸢d⸣[...]

(r 4') Next the mighty woman of the Reš temple, Uṣur-amassu, ... [...].

r 5'5'

EGIR-šu₂ dPAP.SUKKAL dNUSKA dARA u₃ d⸢MES⸣.[SAG.UNUGki ...]

(r 5') Next Papsukkal, Nusku, Usmu and Messagunug [...].

r 6'6'

lu₂⸢NAR⸣-MEŠ lu₂GALA-MEŠ u₃ lu₂MAŠ.MAŠ-MEŠ dINANA x [...]

(r 6') The musicians, the kalû-priests and the mašmaššu-priests [...] Ištar ....

r 7'7'

a-di gišGIGIR d60 1en lu₂KU₄.E₂ ša₂ tug₂IB₂.LA₂ rak-⸢su⸣ [...] x x ⸢u₃? d?⸣[...]

(r 7') Up to the chariot of Anu one temple-enterer who is wearing a girdle [...] ... [...] places [...] on her breast and [...] in the space between the carrying-pole of Ištar. Next Nanaya and Beltu-ša-Reš.

r 8'8'

ina ⸢GABA?⸣-šu₂ GARan-ma ina bi-rit gištal-lu ša₂ d⸢INANA⸣ x x EGIR-šu₂ dna-na-a u ⸢d⸣[GAŠAN—ša₂—SAG]

r 9'9'

EGIR-šu₂ gišGU.ZA ša₂ d60 ša₂ ne₂-me-du dDUMU.MUNUS-MEŠ—⸢UNUG⸣ki u₃ dDUMU.MUNUS-MEŠ—⸢E₂⸣.[AN].⸢NA⸣

(r 9') Next the throne of Anu with a backrest, the Marat-Uruk and the Marat-Eana.

r 10'10'

EGIR-šu₂ dNIN.SI.GAR.AN.NA dNIN.IGI-MIN.ZI.BAR.RA dAB₂.⸢E₂.TUR₃.RA⸣ u₃ dŠA₃.GE.⸢PA₃.DA⸣

(r 10') Next Ninsigarana, Ninigizibara, Abetura and Šagepada.

r 11'11'

EGIR-šu₂ dNIN.ME.UR.UR u₃ dNIN.SUN₂ EGIR-šu₂ d⸢NIN⸣—E₂.AN.NA ša₂ u₄-dan-nu

(r 11') Next Ninmeurur and Ninsun. Next Belet-ayyaki of Udannu.

r 12'12'

[x x d]⸢GAŠAN?⸣—ANe dKA.NI.SUR.RA u₃ d⸢šar-rat—BARA₂⸣ EGIR-šu₂ d⸢a-nun-ni⸣-tu₄ dNIN.IMMA₃

(r 12') [...] Šarrat-šame, Kanisura and Šarrat-parakki. Next Annunitu, Ninimma, [...], Sadarnuna, Šešantur (and) Enmeurana.

r 13'13'

[... dSA].⸢DAR₃⸣.NUN.NA d⸢ŠEŠ⸣.AN.TUR d⸢EN⸣.ME.UR.AN.NA

r 14'14'

[...] DINGIR-MEŠ ki-ma GIR₃-MIN-šu₂-nu ina IGI-šu₂ u EGIR-šu₂ il-lak

(r 14') [...] the gods go in front of her and behind her according to their rank.

r 15'15'

[...] x ša₂? gišMA₂.GUR₈ TE?ḫe?-ma a-na gišGIGIR EREN?.NA

(r 15') He approaches the [...] ... of the processional boat and [...] ... for the chariot (made) of cedar. He goes down with her, and he goes down [...] the sceptre of kingship, and he goes down with her.

r 16'16'

[...] x ⸢it⸣-ti-šu₂ E₁₁-ma gišU₃.LUḪ NAM.LUGAL.[LA ...  ; ...] ⸢ur-rad⸣-ma it-ti-šu₂ ⸢E₁₁?⸣1

r 17'17'

[...] ⸢TUM⸣ dDUMU.MUNUS-MEŠ—UNUGki u₃ dDUMU.MUNUS-MEŠ—E₂.AN.⸢NA⸣

(r 17') [...] ... the Marat-Uruk and the Marat-Eana

r 18'18'

[...] x a-di gišGIGIR-MEŠ it-ti ma-ṣi-ri ina pa-⸢ni⸣-[šu₂]

(r 18') [...] ... up to the chariots with the ... in front of her [...] the carrying pole [...]. As soon as Ištar goes up on the causeway [...], all the gods and the chariots take up position in front of her.

r 19'19'

[... giš]⸢tal-lu⸣ [x x ki]-⸢ma⸣ ša₂ dINANA ina muḫ-ḫi a-ra-⸢am⸣-[mi]

r 20'20'

[... i?-te?]-⸢lu⸣-u₂ DINGIR-MEŠ gab-bi u₃ ⸢gišGIGIR-MEŠ a-na pa-ni-šu₂⸣ LA₂⸢aṣ⸣

r 21'21'

[x] ⸢TUR⸣ BU x U₂ ⸢A⸣ ma-ṣi-ri a-na ŠU-MIN LUGAL GARan-ma ru-ku-bu i-ke-⸢le⸣ d⸢ARA⸣ [x x]

(r 21') [...] .... He places the ... in the hands of the king and moors the boat. Usmu [...] take the hands of the sceptre of kingship and of Ištar and she enters into the forecourt of the temple of the akītu-festival and sits on the great dais in the forecourt of the temple of the akītu-festival. The sceptre of kingship stands at her side.

r 22'22'

⸢ŠU⸣-MIN gišU₃.LUḪ NAM.LUGAL.LA u₃ dINANA DABbat-MEŠ-ma a-na KISAL E₂ a-ki-i-tu₄ KU₄-[ma]

r 23'23'

[x] muḫ-ḫi BARA₂ GAL ina KISAL E₂ a-ki-tu₄ TUŠab gišU₃.LUḪ NAM.LUGAL.LA ina DA-šu₂ GUB⸢za⸣

r 24'24'

⸢DINGIR⸣-MEŠ gab-bi KU₄-MEŠ-ma ina KISAL E₂ a-ki-tu₄ ina pa-ni-šu₂ GUBza šid-di GADA NIGIN-MEŠ-šu₂-[nu-tu₂]

(r 24') All the (other) gods enter and stand in front of her in the forecourt of the temple of the akītu-festival. Linen curtains surround them.

r 25'25'

lu₂KUR.GAR.RA lu₂UR.SAL ša₂ til-le-e dNA.RU.DU rak-su ki-ma maḫ-ri-i TA ⸢2.30⸣

(r 25') The kurgarrû (and) the assinnu cultic performers who are wearing the garments of Narudu circle around them as before from left to right. He raises the water for the hands towards Ištar and he sprinkles the king and the people.

r 26'26'

[a]-na 15 NIGIN-šu₂-nu-tu₂ A-MEŠ ŠU-MIN a-na dINANA IL₂ši-ma LUGAL u₃ UN-MEŠ u₂-lap-⸢pat⸣

r 27'27'

⸢ma⸣-aq-qu-u₂ KU₃.SI₂₂ i-red-de-ma dARA u₃ LUGAL ŠU-MIN dINANA DABbat-ma KU₄-ma ina pa-pa-ḫa-šu₂ TUŠ[ab]

(r 27') He pours (from) the gold libation vessel and Usmu and the king take the hands of Ištar and she enters and sits in her cella.

r 28'28'

giš⸢U₃⸣.LUḪ NAM.LUGAL.LA a-na DA-šu₂ GUBza dGAŠAN—ša₂—SAG KU₄-ma ina pa-pa-ḫa dINANA a-na 2.30 [x x]

(r 28') The sceptre of kingship stands at her side. Beltu-ša-Reš enters and [...] in the cella of Ištar to the left.

r 29'29'

⸢giš⸣GU.ZA ša₂ d60 ša₂ ne₂-me-du u₃ dna-na-a KU₄-MEŠ-ma ina e₂pa-pa-ḫa-šu₂-nu TUŠab DINGIR-MEŠ gab-⸢bi⸣

(r 29') The throne of Anu with a backrest and Nanaya enter and sit in their cella. All the (other) gods and the chariots enter and sit in their seats in the forecourt. The mašmaššu-priest who raises the kappu-bowl to Ištar and all the gods repeatedly pours water. The bronze kettledrum stands on a base [...].

r 30'30'

u₃ gišGIGIR-MEŠ KU₄-MEŠ-ma ina šub-ti-šu₂-nu ša₂ KISAL TUŠab lu₂MAŠ.MAŠ ša₂ kap-pu a-na dINANA u DINGIR-MEŠ ⸢DU₃.A.BI⸣

r 31'31'

⸢IL₂⸣ši A-MEŠ ŠUB.ŠUB-am LI.LI.IZ₃ ZABAR ina muḫ-ḫi šu-uḫ ⸢GUB⸣[za ...]

r 32'32'

[x x] ⸢DAB⸣bat LI.LI.IZ₃ ZABAR ZI-ma gištal-lu KU₃.SI₂₂ ša₂ dINANA x [...]

(r 32') He takes the [...]. The bronze kettledrum arises and the gold carrying-pole of Ištar ... [...].

r 33'33'

[x x] KU₃.SI₂₂ ina pa-ni dINANA uk-tan-nu gišBANŠUR KU₃.BABBAR ina pa-ni DINGIR-MEŠ DU₃.A.⸢BI⸣ [...]

(r 33') The gold [...] is set up in front of Ištar. The silver offering table [...] in front of all the deities.

r 34'34'

[...] x NIG₂.NAqa KU₃.SI₂₂ ina pa-ni dINANA u₂-mal₂-le-e-[ma ...]

(r 34') [...] .... He fills the gold censer in front of Ištar and [...].

r 35'35'

[... KU₃].⸢SI₂₂⸣ ina pa-ni d⸢na⸣-na-a u dGAŠAN—ša₂—SAG u₂-mal₂-⸢le⸣-[e-ma ...]

(r 35') He fills the gold [...] in front of Nanaya and Beltu-ša-Reš and [...].

r 36'36'

[...] x dx [...]

(r 36') [...] ... [...]

(rest of reverse missing)
